stem: Two angles lie on a straight line. One measures $65^\circ$. What is the measure, in degrees, of the other angle?
solutionStandard: Angles on a straight line sum to $180^\circ$: $180 - 65 = 115$.

stem: Two lines intersect, forming an angle of $40^\circ$. What is the measure of the angle vertical to it?
solutionStandard: Vertical angles are always equal, so the vertical angle also measures $40^\circ$.

stem: Two lines intersect. One of the four angles formed is $70^\circ$. What is the measure, in degrees, of an angle adjacent to it?
solutionStandard: Adjacent angles formed by intersecting lines are supplementary: $180 - 70 = 110$.

stem: A transversal crosses two parallel lines, forming an angle of $110^\circ$ with one of them. What is the measure of the corresponding angle at the other parallel line?
solutionStandard: Corresponding angles formed by a transversal crossing parallel lines are always equal, so the corresponding angle is also $110^\circ$.

stem: A transversal crosses two parallel lines. One angle formed is $65^\circ$, and another angle (at the other parallel line) is a co-interior (same-side interior) angle with it.
solutionStandard: Co-interior angles are supplementary: $180 - 65 = 115$. The quantities are equal.

stem: A transversal crosses two parallel lines, creating an angle of $50^\circ$ at the first line. Which of the following angles at the second parallel line must measure $130^\circ$? Select all that apply.

options.text: The angle supplementary to the corresponding angle

solutionStandard: The corresponding angle equals $50^\circ$ (not $130^\circ$), so (A) is false. The co-interior angle is supplementary to $50^\circ$: $180-50=130^\circ$, so (B) is true. The alternate exterior angle equals $50^\circ$ (not $130^\circ$), so (C) is false. Any angle supplementary to the $50^\circ$ corresponding angle is $130^\circ$, so (D) is true.

stem: Three angles around a point measure $x$, $2x$, and $3x$ degrees. What is the value of $x$?
solutionStandard: Angles around a point sum to $360^\circ$: $x + 2x + 3x = 360$, so $6x = 360$ and $x = 60$.

stem: Two angles are supplementary. One angle is $30^\circ$ more than twice the other. What is the measure of the smaller angle?
solutionStandard: Let the smaller angle be $x$. Then $x + (2x + 30) = 180$, so $3x = 150$ and $x = 50$.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The measure of one interior angle is $3x + 10$ and its co-interior (same-side interior) angle is $2x + 20$. What is $x$?
solutionStandard: Co-interior angles are supplementary: $(3x+10) + (2x+20) = 180$, so $5x + 30 = 180$, giving $5x=150$ and $x=30$.

stem: Lines $\ell_1$ and $\ell_2$ intersect at a point, forming four angles. The largest of the four angles is $x$ degrees.
solutionStandard: If the lines are perpendicular, all four angles are $90^\circ$, so $x = 90$. If the lines are not perpendicular, the largest angle exceeds $90^\circ$. Since both cases are possible, the relationship cannot be determined.
